items.question: What does the ROC curve plot?
items.answer: True Positive Rate (TPR = TP/(TP+FN)) on the y-axis vs False Positive Rate (FPR = FP/(FP+TN)) on the x-axis, as the classification threshold on model scores varies from high to low.

items.question: How do you interpret AUC = 0.82?
items.answer: For a random positive and random negative, there is an 82% chance the positive's score ranks higher (ties count 0.5). The classifier separates classes better than random (0.5) but not perfectly (1.0).

items.question: Why is ROC-AUC threshold-independent?
items.answer: It integrates performance over every possible threshold on the score — you compare ranking/separability before committing to one cutoff. Accuracy and F1 at 0.5 embed a single arbitrary threshold.

items.question: When can ROC-AUC be misleading?
items.answer: On heavily imbalanced data when you care about precision on rare positives — ROC can look strong while precision at workable recall is poor. Complement with PR curve, confusion matrix at your operating point, and calibration checks.

items.question: Does ROC-AUC require calibrated probabilities?
items.answer: No — any score where higher values indicate greater confidence in the positive class suffices (probabilities, logits, SVM decision_function). AUC measures ranking, not calibration; monotonic rescaling of scores leaves AUC unchanged.
